title: eriophorum angustifolium var. gracile
intro: Eriophorum angustifolium var. gracile, also known as the slender cotton-grass or narrow-leaved cotton-grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its narrow leaves and a white, cotton-like seed head. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Eriophorum angustifolium var. gracile can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing digestive issues.
